title: Exporting the dates are in the string format
author: vinodhreddygs
body:
Hi,
when i export the dates to excel, the cell format is in the date only but the **date value has the single quote at the beginning**.
Please refer below screen shot for reference.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/37541181/114658833-1eff0880-9d10-11eb-9bf3-0c9dbc2e729c.png)
Please let me know how to solve this issue.
Thanks.

comments.author: cebbens
comments.body:
Hi.

\Don't forget to assign a Date object to that column. Otherwise, it won't format it.

Apart from that, I really don't know why that single quote at the beginning.

I hope you fix it.

comments.author: vinodhreddygs
comments.body:
> Hi.
> 
> \Don't forget to assign a Date object to that column. Otherwise, it won't format it.
> 
> Apart from that, I really don't know why that single quote at the beginning.
> 
> I hope you fix it.

@cebbens actually single quote at the beginning is coming only when i open file in LibreOffice but on the Microsoft excel.
but the real concern in Microsoft excel is that, cell format is in the custom instead of actual Date format.
Please look below screen shoot for reference : 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/37541181/115322423-ab437c80-a1a3-11eb-883d-47e601ee4b64.png)

Code snippet that I'am using for cell format is: 
```
worksheet.columns = [
 { header: 'Submission Date', key: 'Submission_Date(EST)', width: 25, style : { numFmt: 'MM/DD/YYYY' }},
]
```

The file  generated using this npm is : 
[Report (46).xlsx](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/files/6339933/Report.46.xlsx)

comments.author: cebbens
comments.body: The format is okay, and I **guess** that it ends up being Custom (instead of Date) because the format is different than the pre-defined ones and/or different than the one is configured in Excel.

comments.author: vinodhreddygs
comments.body:
> The format is okay, and I **guess** that it ends up being Custom (instead of Date) because the format is different than the pre-defined ones and/or different than the one is configured in Excel.

@cebbens The format which I'am trying to give i.e. MM/DD/YYYY is one of pre-defined formats of excel as you can see in the below screenshot : 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/37541181/115498662-99d59f80-a28b-11eb-9d5e-4249626ba417.png)

By the way whatever format i give, its setting as custom format instead of date in excel.

comments.author: cebbens
comments.body: Got it. So, don't know what else you could do. I'm not an expert nor the author of this lib, just a user. I hope you find the solution :)
